keywords: what can fine flow do for me
answer: Fine Flow is an automated system for managing fines from start to finish. It captures incoming fines, extracts the key details, assigns responsibility, tracks decisions, supports disputes and can even spot an appealable fine and construct the appeal for you — all whilst ensuring everything is resolved on time. Instead of manually chasing emails, checking deadlines and managing drivers, Fine Flow keeps the entire process organised, accountable and under control. Our mission is simple: turning penalties into progress.
follow_up: Want a breakdown of how it all works?

keywords: what value does fine flow provide
answer: Fine Flow provides 24/7 fleet fine tracking, management and compliance in one place. It reduces manual administrative work by up to 80% and ensures no penalty deadlines are ever missed. Fines are automatically captured from your inbox, matched to the right driver, tracked through every stage, and managed all the way to resolution — without your team having to chase a single email.
follow_up: How many vehicles are in your fleet? I can show you exactly what Fine Flow would look like for your operation.

answer: The moment a fine arrives in your connected inbox, Fine Flow picks it up automatically. It reads the email, extracts the key details — amount, PCN reference, vehicle registration, issue date, due date and issuing authority — and creates the fine inside the platform. It then compares this against your driver data and logs using vehicle, date and time matching to identify who was responsible. If a match is found the fine is assigned automatically. If not, it is placed into the unassigned queue for quick manual review so nothing gets missed.
follow_up: Would you like me to walk you through the assignment and dispute stages?

keywords: how does fine flow get fines
answer: Fine Flow connects to your fine-handling Gmail inbox and monitors it every minute. When an email arrives that looks like a penalty notice, the system picks it up automatically, extracts all the relevant details and turns it into a structured record inside the platform. Your team does not need to manually copy anything out of emails. Alternatively an admin can manually upload fines directly if you prefer not to connect an inbox.

keywords: can fineflow pay fines on my behalf
answer: No — and that is intentional. Government and authority payment portals have anti-bot protection, session controls and card verification requirements that make automation impossible and unsafe. Trying to bypass that would be unstable and risky, so payments are always completed by you directly on the authority's site. What Fine Flow does is everything around that step — capturing fines, assigning responsibility, tracking deadlines, flagging urgency — so by the time you reach payment there is no thinking left. Just click the link and complete it.

keywords: can you log in for me
answer: No. Fine Flow does not log into authority websites on your behalf. Payment portals are controlled by external authorities with anti-bot protection, session controls and card verification that make reliable automation unsafe. Fine Flow handles everything up to that final step — capturing fines, extracting details, assigning responsibility, prioritising urgency — so when you are ready to pay you go directly to the correct authority page with everything already prepared.

keywords: how does fine flow know which driver
answer: Fine Flow uses your driver data to work out responsibility automatically. It compares each fine against your uploaded logs checking vehicle registration, date within one day and time window to see who was using that vehicle. When there is a clear match it assigns the fine instantly. If not, it highlights it for review so the process keeps moving without errors or delays. You can assign it in seconds using the vehicle or driver details and the process continues as normal.

If a fine cannot be confidently matched it is placed into either the UNASSIGNED or REVIEW_REQUIRED queue. From there an admin reviews the case and resolves the correct driver assignment based on vehicle registration, timing and driver logs. You can reassign a fine to a different driver at any point — if the initially assigned driver disputes the fine or if there was an error in assignment, the admin updates the driver details to ensure the correct person is responsible.

Yes — Fine Flow does not just manage appeals, it learns from them. When a driver disputes a fine, the admin reviews it and can accept or reject the appeal. If accepted, Fine Flow generates an appeal letter using the fine details and historical outcomes, and sends it directly to the issuing authority. Over time it adapts based on wins and rejections — generating stronger appeal letters, refining its approach and giving you a win probability percentage before you submit so you can make better decisions.
follow_up: Would you like me to explain how the win probability works and how Fine Flow learns from past outcomes?

keywords: does fineflow support all councils appeals
answer: Yes — Fine Flow works with all UK councils and issuing authorities. When an appeal is accepted, Fine Flow generates the appeal letter and sends it directly to whichever authority issued the fine. It does not matter which council or authority it is.

Yes. Every appeal has a tracked status inside Fine Flow. When a driver disputes a fine the status becomes DISPUTED — this is a driver action. If the admin reviews and accepts the dispute, Fine Flow submits the appeal to the authority and the status moves to UNDER REVIEW. From there the outcome is tracked through to ACCEPTED, REJECTED or CANCELLED. Every stage is visible so nothing disappears into an email chain.

The win percentage is a guidance signal, not a guarantee. It reflects how likely a similar appeal is to succeed based on past results, matched patterns and the quality of evidence available. The more real outcomes the system captures over time, the more accurate and useful that percentage becomes. It is there to help you decide whether to submit — not to promise a result.

Fine Flow uses a simple pricing model based on your fleet size. Essential is £99 per month for 5 to 50 vehicles. Core is £199 per month for 51 to 100 vehicles. Advanced is £399 per month for 101 to 200 vehicles. Elite is £499 per month for 200 or more vehicles. Every plan includes a monthly fine allowance with fines charged at £0.75 each within that limit. If you exceed your allowance, additional fines are charged at £2.50 per fine. There is also a pay-as-you-go option at £2.75 per fine with no subscription and no lock-in — ideal for low or unpredictable fine volumes.
follow_up: How many vehicles do you run? I can point you to the right plan straightaway.

keywords: what happens if i go over
answer: If you exceed your plan's monthly fine allowance, each additional fine is charged at £2.50. The £0.75 per-fine rate only applies within your included allowance. Overage charges are collected at the end of your billing cycle. Credits reset to zero at the start of each new billing cycle and unused allowance does not roll over.

Yes. There is a pay-as-you-go option with no monthly subscription fee and zero lock-in. You pay £2.75 per fine as you go — there is no monthly allowance and no subscription. This is a standalone pricing model suited to fleets with low or unpredictable fine volumes who do not want a fixed monthly commitment.

The £0.75 per-fine fee covers the full management of each fine within your monthly allowance — capturing the details, assigning responsibility, tracking deadlines and maintaining the fine record through to its final status. It does not cover Fine Flow organising payment on your behalf — Fine Flow does not manage or process payments. It applies once per fine only and is not charged again for disputes, appeals or reassignments.

Yes — every Fine Flow plan includes the same features with no locked features or paywalls. The only difference between plans is the number of vehicles covered and the monthly fine allowance. Whether you are on Essential or Elite, you have access to the full platform from day one.

They are two completely different pricing models. The £0.75 fee applies when you are on a subscription plan — it is the per-fine rate charged within your monthly allowance. The £2.75 fee is the pay-as-you-go rate and only applies when you have no subscription at all. On PAYG there is no monthly allowance, no subscription and no lock-in — you simply pay £2.75 per fine as you go.

Fine Flow bills monthly via Stripe. Your monthly fine allowance resets to zero at the start of each new billing cycle — unused allowance does not roll over. Fines processed within your allowance are charged at £0.75 each. Any overage beyond your allowance is charged at £2.50 per fine and collected at the end of the cycle. On the pay-as-you-go option there is no monthly fee — you are billed at £2.75 per fine as each one is processed.

If you cancel your Fine Flow account, any fines that were processed during your active subscription period remain chargeable. A cooldown period applies after cancellation — you cannot resubscribe until the end of your current billing period. Any outstanding balance must also be cleared before you can resubscribe.

No — Fine Flow does not store, manage or process card details. All billing is handled externally via Stripe and you use a single payment method per account. Fine Flow never stores raw card data at any point.

keywords: reassigned fine paid is that 2 fines
answer: No — reassigning a fine does not create a second charge. The £0.75 fee applies once when the fine is first processed into the system. Reassigning it to a different driver is an admin action within the same fine record. A fine is only resolved when its final status becomes PAID, OVERDUE or CANCELLED.

keywords: if i appeal and then pay is that 2 charges
answer: No — appealing a fine does not create a second charge. The £0.75 fee applies once when the fine is first processed. The appeal is a separate workflow stage and does not trigger an additional processing fee. A fine is only finally resolved when its status becomes PAID, OVERDUE or CANCELLED.

Yes — an admin can manually upload fines directly into Fine Flow without connecting an inbox. Fine Flow will extract the key details from the uploaded documents. This works alongside the automated email monitoring and gives you flexibility in how fines enter the system.

keywords: what if i don't deal with fine
answer: If a fine is left too long it can escalate and become more expensive. Fine Flow helps prevent that by tracking deadlines, flagging urgent fines and keeping them visible before they become a problem. If a due date is missed the fine is marked OVERDUE so it can be dealt with immediately. Fine Flow does its best to give you plenty of warning well in advance and to push you to act as quickly as possible.

keywords: what should i do right now
answer: Start with urgency. The first things to deal with are fines close to their deadline, fines that are already overdue, or fines about to lose a discount window. After that clear anything unassigned or awaiting confirmation so nothing is left hanging. Fine Flow helps you focus on the next most important action, not just show you data.

keywords: how many fines do i have
answer: Your current fines, including anything pending, unassigned or overdue, are all visible on your Fine Flow dashboard. Log in and head to the dashboard — it will show you exactly what is outstanding and what needs attention first. If you tell me what you are seeing I can help you prioritise.

Yes. Fine Flow is fully GDPR compliant. It uses JWT authentication with 24-hour token expiry, bcrypt password hashing and AES-256-CBC encryption for Gmail tokens and app passwords. Fine Flow never sells or shares data with third parties — your data is yours. Payments are handled externally via Stripe and Fine Flow never stores raw card details at any point.

No — Fine Flow never stores your card details. Card data is handled entirely by Stripe, which is an external payment processor. Fine Flow does not see, store or process raw card information at any point. Your payment information stays within Stripe's secure environment.

The company dashboard focuses on urgency and action — it shows outstanding fines, anything overdue or unassigned, appeals in progress, credit balance, billing status and time saved. The driver dashboard is separate and personal to each driver — it shows their own fines, points on licence, appeals in progress, safe driving streak and 12-month fine history. Drivers can only see their own data.

Yes — Fine Flow has an active referral programme. When a company signs up using your referral code and subscribes, you earn bonus credits based on their fleet size: 100 credits for 1 to 25 vehicles, 250 for 26 to 100, 750 for 101 to 500 and 2,000 for 500 or more. As you hit referral milestones you unlock subscription discounts — Gold at 5 referrals gives 10% off for 12 months, Platinum at 10 gives 15% off, and Titan at 25 gives 20% off for life. Any new company joining with a referral code gets £75 in credits on subscribing.

No. Driver logs improve automation and make matching much more accurate but they are not a hard requirement. If logs are missing Fine Flow still captures, tracks and organises fines — it simply means more assignment work is handled manually. The system remains useful with imperfect data, not only with ideal data.
You can reach the Fine Flow team by phone on +47 32 28 50 00 or by email at ff.sales@fineflow.com. Fine Flow has offices in Edinburgh, Glasgow, Belfast, Manchester, London, Dublin and Hamburg.
